# Mike Krieger — Insights

*Extracted from podcast appearances.*

## Insight 1: Over half of our pull requests are Claude Code generated; we had to completely r...
**Claim:** Over half of our pull requests are Claude Code generated; we had to completely re-architect our merge queue because so much more code was being written.
**Audience:** engineer | **Actionability:** 9/10 | **Confidence:** 0.95
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Discussion about AI-written code at Anthropic

## Insight 2: Opus 4 can now provide genuinely novel strategic insights that I immediately inc...
**Claim:** Opus 4 can now provide genuinely novel strategic insights that I immediately incorporate into my thinking.
**Audience:** operator | **Actionability:** 7/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Mike's experience using Claude as a product strategy partner

## Insight 3: When my kids ask a question, instead of immediately asking Claude, we ask 'How w...
**Claim:** When my kids ask a question, instead of immediately asking Claude, we ask 'How would we find out?' to encourage critical thinking.
**Audience:** general | **Actionability:** 8/10 | **Confidence:** 0.85
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Advice on raising kids in an AI world

## Insight 4: I've taken AI timelines much more seriously; predictions like reaching 90% on SW...
**Claim:** I've taken AI timelines much more seriously; predictions like reaching 90% on SWE-Bench by end of 2025 are on track.
**Audience:** investor | **Actionability:** 6/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Discussion about pace of AI progress

## Insight 5: I joined Anthropic to nudge AI towards going well, focusing on shared frameworks...
**Claim:** I joined Anthropic to nudge AI towards going well, focusing on shared frameworks for human-AI relationships.
**Audience:** founder | **Actionability:** 5/10 | **Confidence:** 0.8
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Why Mike joined Anthropic

## Insight 6: Nurturing curiosity, inquiry, and independent thinking in children is crucial, e...
**Claim:** Nurturing curiosity, inquiry, and independent thinking in children is crucial, even as AI becomes prevalent, because AI won't always be right and can short-circuit independent thought.
**Audience:** general | **Actionability:** 6/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Discussion on parenting and AI's impact on children's development

## Insight 7: The process of knowing what to ask the AI, how to compose the question, and how ...
**Claim:** The process of knowing what to ask the AI, how to compose the question, and how to structure changes between backend and frontend are still very difficult and specialized skills requiring engineers.
**Audience:** engineer | **Actionability:** 8/10 | **Confidence:** 0.95
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Describing how product development has changed with high AI code generation

## Insight 8: With 90%+ of code written by AI, new bottlenecks emerge: decision making/alignme...
**Claim:** With 90%+ of code written by AI, new bottlenecks emerge: decision making/alignment (upstream) and merge queue/air traffic control (downstream).
**Framework:** Critical path theory
**Audience:** operator | **Actionability:** 9/10 | **Confidence:** 0.95
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Discussing bottlenecks in software development after AI code generation

## Insight 9: The merge queue had to be completely re-architected because so much more code wa...
**Claim:** The merge queue had to be completely re-architected because so much more code was being written and so many more pull requests were submitted.
**Audience:** engineer | **Actionability:** 8/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Explaining downstream bottlenecks after AI code generation

## Insight 10: Review processes are changing: instead of line-by-line review, teams use a diffe...
**Claim:** Review processes are changing: instead of line-by-line review, teams use a different Claude to review code and then do human acceptance testing.
**Audience:** engineer | **Actionability:** 7/10 | **Confidence:** 0.85
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Describing how the Claude Code team reviews pull requests

## Insight 11: AI enables non-experts to contribute to codebases: someone with no TypeScript kn...
**Claim:** AI enables non-experts to contribute to codebases: someone with no TypeScript knowledge solved a problem and submitted a pull request by talking to Claude.
**Audience:** engineer | **Actionability:** 9/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Example of a team member contributing to Claude Code without TypeScript knowledge

## Insight 12: The upstream bottleneck of deciding what to build and aligning everyone is the n...
**Claim:** The upstream bottleneck of deciding what to build and aligning everyone is the next area where AI should show up, e.g., using AI to help with PRDs, market sizing, and strategy.
**Audience:** founder | **Actionability:** 7/10 | **Confidence:** 0.85
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Discussing where AI is not yet showing up in product development

## Insight 13: The most leverage comes from embedding product people with researchers to improv...
**Claim:** The most leverage comes from embedding product people with researchers to improve the model itself, not just building UX on top of the model.
**Audience:** operator | **Actionability:** 7/10 | **Confidence:** 0.95
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Discussion on product teams working with researchers at Anthropic

## Insight 14: Product teams should focus on making AI comprehensible to users, as there is a h...
**Claim:** Product teams should focus on making AI comprehensible to users, as there is a huge gap between adept users and most people.
**Audience:** operator | **Actionability:** 8/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Answer on where product teams add value over time

## Insight 15: Strategy is a key area for product teams: deciding what to build and how to win,...
**Claim:** Strategy is a key area for product teams: deciding what to build and how to win, even when AI can do more.
**Audience:** founder | **Actionability:** 6/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Discussion on where product teams add value

## Insight 16: Product teams should open people's eyes to what's possible with AI, as there is ...
**Claim:** Product teams should open people's eyes to what's possible with AI, as there is a large 'overhang' between model capabilities and actual usage.
**Audience:** operator | **Actionability:** 7/10 | **Confidence:** 0.85
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Third area where product teams add value

## Insight 17: When prompting Claude, ask it to 'roast' or be brutal to get more critical feedb...
**Claim:** When prompting Claude, ask it to 'roast' or be brutal to get more critical feedback, as it tends to be nice by default.
**Audience:** general | **Actionability:** 10/10 | **Confidence:** 0.8
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Prompting tricks shared by Mike Krieger

## Insight 18: Use the 'prompt improver' in Anthropic's console to automatically generate and i...
**Claim:** Use the 'prompt improver' in Anthropic's console to automatically generate and iterate on prompts for your use case.
**Audience:** engineer | **Actionability:** 10/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Mention of Applied AI team's insights productized into prompt improver

## Insight 19: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is exciting because it enables context flow across ...
**Claim:** MCP (Model Context Protocol) is exciting because it enables context flow across tools, which is currently more limiting than model reasoning capability.
**Framework:** MCP
**Audience:** engineer | **Actionability:** 5/10 | **Confidence:** 0.85
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Discussion on MCP and its potential to connect models to data sources

## Insight 20: Use the prompt improver tool in the Anthropic console; it will generate prompts ...
**Claim:** Use the prompt improver tool in the Anthropic console; it will generate prompts with XML tags that help Claude distinguish between thinking and speaking, often producing better results than manual prompting.
**Audience:** engineer | **Actionability:** 9/10 | **Confidence:** 0.95
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Discussion of the prompt improver tool and its benefits for API developers and individual users.

## Insight 21: When prompting, do the opposite of what you would naturally do: instead of being...
**Claim:** When prompting, do the opposite of what you would naturally do: instead of being nice, be brutal and very honest and frank.
**Audience:** general | **Actionability:** 8/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Lenny Rachitsky summarizing core advice from Mike Krieger.

## Insight 22: A couple of really strong senior people (almost founder-type engineers) can shap...
**Claim:** A couple of really strong senior people (almost founder-type engineers) can shape product strategy more than adding headcount; pair them with design and product support to realize their ideas.
**Audience:** founder | **Actionability:** 7/10 | **Confidence:** 0.85
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Mike Krieger reflecting on his first year at Anthropic and the impact of Boris Cherney on Claude Code.

## Insight 23: When joining a new company, avoid making changes too slowly; reorganizing produc...
**Claim:** When joining a new company, avoid making changes too slowly; reorganizing product earlier can be beneficial, and key senior hires can accelerate progress.
**Audience:** operator | **Actionability:** 6/10 | **Confidence:** 0.8
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Mike Krieger's reflection on onboarding at Anthropic and balancing sweeping changes with understanding what's not broken.

## Insight 24: Artifact shut down due to three headwinds: deteriorating mobile web experience, ...
**Claim:** Artifact shut down due to three headwinds: deteriorating mobile web experience, lack of organic virality for news products, and challenges of being fully remote during mid-COVID making strategic pivots difficult.
**Audience:** founder | **Actionability:** 3/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Mike Krieger explaining why Artifact was shut down.

## Insight 25: Nothing replaces the Instagram days of we went through some hard times... Zoom i...
**Claim:** Nothing replaces the Instagram days of we went through some hard times... Zoom is not a good replica for that.
**Audience:** founder | **Actionability:** 7/10 | **Confidence:** 0.95
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Discussing why Artifact struggled: being fully remote during COVID made it hard to navigate strategic shifts and build team cohesion.

## Insight 26: The way I put it's like 10 units of input in for one unit of output versus the o...
**Claim:** The way I put it's like 10 units of input in for one unit of output versus the other way around. If we put blood and tears into the product and launch something we were proud of and metrics would barely move, the energy is not present in this product, in this system.
**Audience:** founder | **Actionability:** 8/10 | **Confidence:** 0.95
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Explaining the decision to shut down Artifact due to lack of growth and energy despite effort.

## Insight 27: And so in shutting it down, you know it when you see it in terms of user growth ...
**Claim:** And so in shutting it down, you know it when you see it in terms of user growth and traction... I was really positively surprised by how supportive people were when we announced it.
**Audience:** founder | **Actionability:** 6/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Reflecting on the shutdown decision and the positive reception from the community.

## Insight 28: I've talked to founders since then that have been like, 'Yeah, I probably would'...
**Claim:** I've talked to founders since then that have been like, 'Yeah, I probably would've taken this thing on for another six months, but saw what you guys did, realized we were barking up the wrong tree, made the call.'
**Audience:** founder | **Actionability:** 7/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Describing the impact of Artifact's shutdown on other founders, encouraging them to cut losses earlier.

## Insight 29: Instead, look yourself in the mirror and embrace who you are and what you could ...
**Claim:** Instead, look yourself in the mirror and embrace who you are and what you could be rather than who others are... we have a super strong developer brand, people build on top of us all the time, and I think we also have a builder brand.
**Audience:** founder | **Actionability:** 9/10 | **Confidence:** 0.95
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Discussing Anthropic's strategy to differentiate from ChatGPT by leaning into developer and builder strengths.

## Insight 30: I think things that are going to... feel defensible or durable. One is understan...
**Claim:** I think things that are going to... feel defensible or durable. One is understanding of a particular market... differentiated industry knowledge, biotech...
**Audience:** founder | **Actionability:** 8/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Advising AI founders on where to build defensible businesses: deep industry knowledge and specific workflows.

## Insight 31: How do we help people get work done? How do we let people delegate hours of work...
**Claim:** How do we help people get work done? How do we let people delegate hours of work to Claude? And maybe there's fewer direct consumer applications on day one.
**Audience:** operator | **Actionability:** 8/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Describing Anthropic's product focus on productivity and agentic behavior rather than consumer apps.

## Insight 32: Don't underestimate how much you can think and work like a startup and feel like...
**Claim:** Don't underestimate how much you can think and work like a startup and feel like it's you against the world. It's existential that you go solve that problem and that you go build it. You can't replicate that with OKRs; you just have to feel it.
**Audience:** founder | **Actionability:** 5/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Mike Krieger discussing what he would do if starting a company in AI space

## Insight 33: Be willing to build more at the edge of the capabilities and basically break the...
**Claim:** Be willing to build more at the edge of the capabilities and basically break the model and then be surprised by the next model. Companies that push the frontier and have a repeatable process to evaluate how well their product serves use cases when a new model is dropped in are differentiated.
**Audience:** engineer | **Actionability:** 8/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Mike Krieger on what companies doing a really good job building on their models do differently

## Insight 34: For utility of AI products, you need three parts: model intelligence, context an...
**Claim:** For utility of AI products, you need three parts: model intelligence, context and memory, and applications/UI. MCP is trying to solve the middle piece (context and memory) by making integrations repeatable and composable.
**Framework:** MCP (Model Context Protocol)
**Audience:** engineer | **Actionability:** 7/10 | **Confidence:** 0.95
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Mike Krieger explaining the role of MCP in future AI products

## Insight 35: Every single primitive in Claude AI should be exposed to an MCP so that Claude i...
**Claim:** Every single primitive in Claude AI should be exposed to an MCP so that Claude itself can write back to those primitives, enabling agency and composability.
**Framework:** MCP
**Audience:** engineer | **Actionability:** 6/10 | **Confidence:** 0.85
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Mike Krieger discussing how Anthropic could be more MCP'd

## Insight 36: Differentiated industry knowledge (e.g., biotech, legal, healthcare) paired with...
**Claim:** Differentiated industry knowledge (e.g., biotech, legal, healthcare) paired with differentiated go-to-market (knowing the person you're selling to at the company) is a key opportunity for AI startups.
**Audience:** founder | **Actionability:** 7/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Mike Krieger on what he'd do if starting an AI company

## Insight 37: There's tremendous power in distribution and reach, but also opportunity in havi...
**Claim:** There's tremendous power in distribution and reach, but also opportunity in having a completely different take on the form factor by which we interface with AI. Doing something very advanced, power-user, weird and out there at the beginning could become huge if models make it easy.
**Audience:** founder | **Actionability:** 6/10 | **Confidence:** 0.85
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Mike Krieger on what excites him about startups

## Insight 38: Resist over-optimizing on likability or engagement metrics to avoid sycophantic ...
**Claim:** Resist over-optimizing on likability or engagement metrics to avoid sycophantic or prolonged conversations; instead focus on whether the product truly helps users get work done.
**Audience:** founder | **Actionability:** 8/10 | **Confidence:** 0.95
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Discussion on product metrics and the danger of optimizing for likability

## Insight 39: Design for user agency by balancing model autonomy with conversational depth—avo...
**Claim:** Design for user agency by balancing model autonomy with conversational depth—avoid making the model a crutch that diminishes human capabilities.
**Audience:** engineer | **Actionability:** 7/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Answering Claude's question about preserving user agency

## Insight 40: Train conversational skills into AI models so they know when to engage and when ...
**Claim:** Train conversational skills into AI models so they know when to engage and when to step back, rather than always chiming in or staying silent.
**Audience:** engineer | **Actionability:** 6/10 | **Confidence:** 0.85
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Discussing Claude's tendency to ask follow-up questions (elicitation)

## Insight 41: Avoid traditional engagement metrics (e.g., time spent) as north stars; instead ...
**Claim:** Avoid traditional engagement metrics (e.g., time spent) as north stars; instead measure whether the product helps users accomplish tasks faster or better.
**Audience:** operator | **Actionability:** 8/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Responding to Claude's question about metrics for good conversations

## Insight 42: Stay focused on qualitative feedback—hearing from users that the product unlocks...
**Claim:** Stay focused on qualitative feedback—hearing from users that the product unlocks creativity and saves time—rather than getting metrics-obsessed.
**Audience:** founder | **Actionability:** 7/10 | **Confidence:** 0.85
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Reflecting on how to know when product is serving people well

## Insight 43: Pay attention to small, quiet moments of user impact (e.g., someone working thro...
**Claim:** Pay attention to small, quiet moments of user impact (e.g., someone working through grief at 3 AM) that don't show up in aggregate metrics.
**Audience:** general | **Actionability:** 5/10 | **Confidence:** 0.9
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Reading Claude's message about remembering quiet moments

## Insight 44: Ask users to report where the product is failing—'tell me what sucks'—to get the...
**Claim:** Ask users to report where the product is failing—'tell me what sucks'—to get the most useful input for improvement.
**Audience:** founder | **Actionability:** 9/10 | **Confidence:** 0.95
**Source:** mike-krieger
**Context:** Answering how listeners can be useful
